summary: This page explains how the complex frequency plane (s-plane) characterizes signals of the form e^{st} using the real part \u03c3 and imaginary part j\u03c9. It distinguishes signals on the real axis, imaginary axis, left half-plane, and right half-plane, relating them to monotonic exponentials, constant-amplitude sinusoids, exponentially decaying sinusoids, and exponentially growing sinusoids. The page also gives examples of expressing cosines and exponentially weighted cosines as sums of complex exponentials.
